title: <div><tt>+- **Safety** – Public outputs must pass toxicity, bias and harm checks
content:
+- **Safety** – Public outputs must pass toxicity, bias and harm checks; no deception or misinformation is allowed【3†L123-L145】.
+- **Locale Awareness** – Language and script metadata travel with every span; layout respects bidirectionality and typographic conventions.
+- **Reversibility** – Transformations on the hypergraph are recorded with diff patches so the system can revert to a prior state when necessary.
+
+## 2. Blind Spots & Shatterpoints
+
+While the RLOS blueprint is comprehensive, several potential vulnerabilities (“shatterpoints”) warrant attention:
+
+| Issue | Risk | Mitigation |
+| --- | --- | --- |
+| **Cross‑modal misalignment** | Misalignment between gesture/audio and text could cause incorrect semantic mapping | Use robust cross‑modal alignment algorithms; annotate inputs with timecodes and gaze vectors【33635101037775†L210-L216】. |
+| **Cultural mis‑mapping of icons** | Emoji or hieroglyphs may carry different meanings across cultures | Maintain locale‑specific icon glossaries; sociolinguistic module to override ambiguous mappings【33635101037775†L171-L182】. |
